configured_rows.host_separation_rule: Sandbox uses its own non-production hosts and never shares them with pre-production or production.

source_refs.rationale: HMRC redirect URI rules govern registered callback patterns.

configured_rows.host_separation_rule: Sandbox native loopback path is port-and-path segregated from pre-production and production native callbacks.

source_refs.rationale: HMRC installed-application redirect rules govern native loopback callbacks.

configured_rows.rationale: Stable pre-production web callback for release-candidate verification.
configured_rows.host_separation_rule: Pre-production keeps a production-like but still sandbox-only callback surface.

source_refs.rationale: Pre-production verification needs stable sandbox-backed callback hosts.

configured_rows.rationale: Stable pre-production native callback for release-candidate verification.
configured_rows.host_separation_rule: Pre-production native loopback callback uses a dedicated path and port range distinct from sandbox and production.

source_refs.rationale: Native operator flows stay system-browser managed while server truth remains authoritative.

disallowed_rows.rationale: Local browser loopback is non-promotable and does not represent Taxat's canonical web callback posture.
disallowed_rows.host_separation_rule: Local provisioning loopback only; never register for production.

source_refs.rationale: The canonical Taxat web callback remains HTTPS and gateway-owned.

disallowed_rows.rationale: Local provisioning native loopback is bootstrap-only and not worth spending a stable sandbox-app slot on.
disallowed_rows.host_separation_rule: Local native loopback only; reserved for sandbox bootstrap and native auth-session testing.

source_refs.rationale: Installed-application localhost remains supported but this specific URI is not the canonical runtime callback.

disallowed_rows.rationale: Production callback hosts must remain on the separate HMRC production application.
disallowed_rows.host_separation_rule: Production must remain disjoint from every non-production callback domain.

source_refs.rationale: Production promotion freezes live provider trust separately from sandbox.

disallowed_rows.rationale: Production desktop callback belongs on the HMRC production application, not on the sandbox app.
disallowed_rows.host_separation_rule: Production native callback uses a dedicated port and path distinct from all non-production callbacks.

source_refs.rationale: Production provider trust is a separate governed boundary.

future_slot_policy: Keep one redirect slot open. Do not allocate it without a later card that proves a new sandbox-side callback is both canonical and unavoidable.
typed_gaps: HMRC's user-restricted guidance currently documents sandbox authorisation at https://test-www.tax.service.gov.uk/oauth/authorize and token exchange at https://test-api.service.hmrc.gov.uk/oauth/token, while current API OAS files still publish https://api.service.hmrc.gov.uk/oauth/authorize and /oauth/token in their security schemes. Taxat follows the user-restricted guidance for sandbox runtime endpoint selection and keeps the discrepancy explicit.
notes: The sandbox app budgets for both shared-sandbox and pre-production callback surfaces because both product environments bind to HMRC sandbox.
Local provisioning callbacks and all production callbacks remain explicitly out of budget for this sandbox app.
